channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: More Americans jailed in Venezuela pose a test of Trump's deal-making foreign policy
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro will be sworn in for a third term Friday while hundreds of government opponents arrested since his disputed reelection last summer languish in the country's packed prisons. Sharing cells with them are as many as 10 Americans.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostage found dead in Gaza, military says
item.description: Israeli forces have recovered the body of a hostage held in Gaza, the military said Wednesday, adding that it was identifying additional remains that could belong to another captive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Days before Venezuela’s presidential inauguration, self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González was in Panama Wednesday rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeffries: 'Confused' by Trump's 'obsession' with Greenland
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) hammered President-elect Trump on Wednesday for what he characterized as an “obsession” with the idea of the U.S. taking over Greenland, accusing Trump of fixating on an issue of no consequence for working-class Americans.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Imprisoned Belarus activist resurfaces after being held incommunicado for over 700 days
item.description: An imprisoned opposition leader in Belarus has resurfaced in a video shot by a pro-government blogger after over 700 days of no contact with his family.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France says EU won't tolerate attack on borders after Trump Greenland takeover remarks
item.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot says the European Union (EU) will stand firm in its alliance in the face of repeated comments from President-elect Trump about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: House Republican: Gulf of Mexico name less important than Trump's actions
item.description: Rep. Austin Scott (R-Ga.) said President-elect Trump’s musing about changing the name of the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America is less important than his actions in the region, particularly when it comes to curbing illegal fishing and drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A daytime Russian missile attack on the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia killed at least 13 civilians and injured about 30 others Wednesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to appoint Eric Trager as Middle East director for National Security Council
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to announce Eric Trager as Senior Director for the Middle East and North Africa on the National Security Council, a congressional aide told The Hill. Trager has served as a professional staff member for Republicans on the Senate Armed Services Committee since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tents arrive for survivors of a quake that killed 126 in freezing, high-altitude Tibet
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Relief teams in western China shifted their focus to resettling survivors after a search Wednesday for any remaining victims of a deadly earthquake that struck a day earlier near a holy city for Tibetan Buddhists.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala freed from detention in Iran
item.description: Italian journalist Cecilia Sala was released after being detained in Iran for three weeks. Sala's plight became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis has named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, choosing an Italian nun
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Monday named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, appointing an Italian nun, Sister Simona Brambilla, to become prefect of the department responsible for all the Catholic Church's religious orders.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hostages in Gaza endure another winter as their families plead for a ceasefire
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When Luis Har was kidnapped by Hamas-led militants on the warm morning of Oct. 7, 2023, he was forced into Gaza wearing shorts and a T-shirt.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Greenland? Remote but resource-rich island occupies a key position in a warming world
item.description: Remote, icy and mostly pristine, Greenland plays an outsized role in the daily weather experienced by billions of people and in the climate changes taking shape all over the planet.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What would Bonhoeffer do? Anti-Nazi pastor's legacy claimed, debated across political spectrum
item.description: Shortly before he was hanged by the Nazis in 1945 at age 39, Dietrich Bonhoeffer reportedly told a fellow prisoner: “This is the end — for me the beginning of life.” It was – more than he knew.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened 'all hell will break out' if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau says there ‘isn’t a snowball’s chance in hell’ Canada will become part of US
item.description: Canadian prime minister Justin Trudeau said in an X post on Tuesday that there is not a "snowball's chance in hell" Canada will become part of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants left stranded in troubled resort as Mexico disperses them far from US border
item.description: ACAPULCO, Mexico (AP) — About 100 migrants from various countries wandered directionless and disoriented through the streets of the troubled Pacific coast resort of Acapulco.
item.dc:creator: ANTONIO CASTILLO and MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim Jong Un’s big guns spotted on Russian front lines: report
item.description: A North Korean howitzer has been reported on Russia's front lines following November sightings of a shipment of two M1989 Koksan self-propelled big guns as Pyongyang entered the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France marks 10 years since deadly Charlie Hebdo attack
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A decade after Islamic extremist gunmen stormed the offices of the satirical newspaper Charlie Hebdo in a deadly assault that shook France to its core and ignited a global outcry in defense of freedom of speech, the nation paused on Tuesday to honor the victims and renew its resolve to
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Controversial founder of French far right, Jean-Marie Le Pen, dies aged 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, who founded France's far-right National Front party, died at 96 years old. Le Pen was a controversial and confrontational figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claimed victory over Maduro meets with Biden
item.description: Edmundo González Urrutia, the Venezuelan opposition leader who claimed to have beaten current President Nicolás Maduro in an election in July, met with President Biden on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Pierre Poilievre? Canada's Conservative leader seeking to become next prime minister after Trudeau exit
item.description: The leader of Canada's Conservative party, Pierre Poilievre, has been leading in the polls and is favorite to become the country's next prime minister following the exit of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation
